methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $400M - $2.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Umbra is a Series B-stage Space & Aerospace company that has raised $100M+ to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comp universe of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers operating in the same sector and stage.

Two quantitative methods prove applicable. Stage-progression anchors to the $100M raised proxy and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implying $190M. Peer-median comparison draws on three directly comparable Series B Space & Aerospace companies: Cowboy Space Corporation ($2.0B), Aalyria ($1.3B), and Hubble Network ($400M). The median across these three peers is $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because Umbra's revenue is not disclosed; the sector median P/S of 64.2x across five public comparables cannot be deployed without that input.

The two applicable methods produce a wide range. Stage-progression yields $190M, while peer-median yields $1.3B. The triangulated valuation range is $162M (low), $745M (median), and $1.5B (high). The dispersion reflects meaningful uncertainty around whether Umbra's stage trajectory and revenue profile align more closely with earlier-stage or later-stage comparables. Revenue visibility and near-term customer traction represent key derisking variables for narrowing this range at the next fundraise.
